methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: The Rollup contract's token balance must always be greater than or equal to the sum of all active stakes plus total withdrawable funds.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract balance falls below its liabilities, stakers will be unable to withdraw their funds, resulting in protocol insolvency and loss of user funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_rollup_solvency() public view { uint256 totalLiabilities = totalStaked + totalWithdrawableFunds; assert(stakeToken.balanceOf(address(this)) >= totalLiabilities); }

invariants.description: Only one edge per mutual ID can ever be confirmed, ensuring that only one stake is refunded per dispute.
invariants.attack_scenario: If multiple rival edges are confirmed, multiple stakes could be refunded from a single dispute, draining the challenge manager's funds and breaking the zero-sum nature of the challenge game.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_single_confirmed_rival(bytes32 mutualId) public view { bytes32 confirmedId = store.confirmedRivals[mutualId]; if (confirmedId != bytes32(0)) { assert(store.edges[confirmedId].status == EdgeStatus.Confirmed); } }

invariants.description: When a sibling assertion is created (a fork in the rollup state), the required stake must be transferred to the loserStakeEscrow.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the stake is not transferred to the escrow, honest validators will not be compensated for the gas and opportunity cost of defending against the invalid sibling assertion.
invariants.invariant_logic: balanceOf(loserStakeEscrow) >= sum(requiredStake of all non-first-child assertions)

invariants.description: A staker cannot withdraw their stake or reduce it below the required amount if they are actively defending an unconfirmed leaf without children.
invariants.attack_scenario: An attacker could stake, create an invalid assertion, and immediately withdraw their stake before being challenged, executing a costless 'nothing-at-stake' griefing attack.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_active_staker_locked(address staker) public view { if (isStaked(staker) && !isInactive(staker)) { assert(amountStaked(staker) >= baseStake); } }

invariants.description: The cached total time unrivaled for an edge must be strictly monotonically increasing.
invariants.attack_scenario: If an attacker can decrease the unrivaled time of an honest edge, they can indefinitely delay its confirmation, stalling the rollup and preventing honest validators from winning challenges.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_timer_monotonicity(bytes32 edgeId, uint256 oldTimer) public { updateTimerCache(edgeId); assert(store.edges[edgeId].totalTimeUnrivaledCache >= oldTimer); }

invariants.description: The global totalWithdrawableFunds variable must exactly equal the sum of all individual user withdrawable funds.
invariants.attack_scenario: If totalWithdrawableFunds desyncs from individual balances, it could mask a vulnerability where users are credited more funds than the contract accounts for, leading to insolvency when multiple users attempt to withdraw.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_total_withdrawable() public view { assert(totalWithdrawableFunds == sum_all_withdrawable()); }
